mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, and others. It provides a straightforward way to deploy and interact with these models via a command-line interface and REST API. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the flexibility of custom configurations.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary to interact with the models.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command fetches the credentials for your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/ollama-deployment.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the deployment configuration for Ollama.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Ollama deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment ollama --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/ollama-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command sets up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> This YAML file contains the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama from Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment and associated resources from your cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/ollama-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command deletes the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama Deployment
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade the Ollama deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -l app=ollama -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/ollama-deployment.yaml

kubectl get pods -l app=ollama

```

> This command drains the pods before upgrading.
> Reapply the deployment configuration to upgrade.
> Verify that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get pods -l app=ollama -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama Docker image may not be available or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and verify the image name with 'docker pull ollama/ollama:latest'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama application may be misconfigured or lacking necessary resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource limits in the deployment YAML.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the correct roles assigned with 'kubectl create clusterrolebinding <binding-name> --clusterrole=cluster-admin --serviceaccount=<namespace>:<service-account>'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig and ensure the cluster is up and running with 'gcloud container clusters list'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including MiniMax and DeepSeek, improved REST API performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' showing the Ollama pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
